With A.I. Glasses, Korea’s Theaters Hope for a K-Pop Moment

A growing number of South Korean theater productions are offering glasses that display translated subtitles in their lenses. I used the glasses for the first time at “Finding Mr. Destiny” in Daehangno, Seoul’s theater district.
Producers and cultural authorities alike hope that the technology can turn Korean theater into a force as powerful as Korea’s music, film and beauty industries.
Most of the people I talked to at the show had fairly positive reviews of the glasses, but, as someone who understands both English and Korean, I found myself nitpicking at areas for improvement.

Several producers said the glasses have already succeeded in drawing in non-Korean audiences. Looking more broadly, experts expressed cautious optimism about their potential to change live performances.
